data.body:
Verification summary (2026-04-27):

## Outbound sync (local → Jira) — VERIFIED

- CREATE: 5dc3-0d5e → DIG-1573, correct title + description confirmed via ACLI ✅
- COMMENT: Posted to DIG-1573 with origin-uuid echo-prevention marker ✅
- STATUS (closed → Done): DIG-1574 created with status 'Done' ✅
- Assignee validation fix (0cd6-57d6): 'Test' assignee skipped gracefully, bridge retried without assignee ✅

## Inbound sync (Jira → local) — PREVIOUSLY VERIFIED IN CI

- TZ fix (275d-dfd5): 6-hour lookback fetched 1 issue post-fix (confirmed CI run 25029128946) ✅
- INBOUND_TYPE_MAPPING: configured + verified (fetched=2, unmapped_type=0, create_events=2) ✅
- verify_jira_timezone_utc fix (6286-cb8e): closed ✅

## Remaining items (blocked/deferred)

1. Inbound EDIT verification: tickets-tracker-guard hook blocks local bridge run; needs CI run
2. Checkpoint rewind to 2026-04-16T16:13:00Z: blocked by tickets-tracker-guard on .tickets-tracker/.inbound-checkpoint.json; requires direct commit to tickets branch
3. Historical Jira issues from 2026-04-16 to 2026-04-23 may not be in local tickets without the rewind

## New bug discovered during verification

COMPACT event triggers outbound bridge re-CREATE: when a ticket is compacted (COMPACT commit replaces individual events + SYNC file), the bridge processes the SNAPSHOT file as a new CREATE, losing the prior Jira key mapping and creating a duplicate issue. Filed as separate bug.
